programmes.show_description: (1982) Sidney J Furie's seminal horror stars Barbara Hershey as a divorced mother of three who becomes the victim of a poltergeist that submits her to multiple violent and sexual assaults.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1941) Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara and Roddy McDowall star in John Ford's multi-Oscar-winning period drama set in the mining communities of the South Wales Valleys of the 19th century.
(1959) Comedy western with Kenneth More. An English gunsmith decides the Wild West is the best market for his wares, but little does he expect to end up sheriff of a lawless frontier town.
(1956) Western. Richard Widmark plays a man who murdered the killers of his wife and children and who becomes the unlikely saviour of a wagon train that's attacked by Apaches.
(1962) War drama. Darryl F Zanuck's classic Oscar-winning recreation of the Allied landings in Normandy on 6 June 1944, with an all-star British, American, German and French cast.
(2010) Action-comedy starring Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz. A CIA assassin kidnaps a woman and takes her on an impromptu round-the-world trip in order to save both their lives. Violence.
(1979) Final cut of Coppola's award-laden take on the Vietnam War, with Martin Sheen and Marlon Brando. An assassin travels upriver to terminate a renegade colonel with extreme prejudice.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1966) This film's sci-fi, not auteur. Pint-sized medics become voyageurs. Add oceanological; to haematological. Not to mention a hidd'n saboteur. #WorldPoetryDay
(1957) Scott's getting smaller; a nuclear thing. The cat getting bolder, scaring him. Lives in a dollhouse. Longs for a friend. Questions his sanity. Will his shrinking end? #WorldPoetryDay
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(2001) Screwball farce with a well-known cast. There's $2m at stake to be first across the States, while John Cleese bets on all the chases. It's all a bit like Wacky Races. #WorldPoetryDay
(2020) Drama set in Italy. A widower and a divorcee. Father's Robert, Jack's his son. Both estranged since Jack was young. Bonding on a renovation: Tuscan reconciliation. #WorldPoetryDay
(2022) A whodunnit worthy of Agatha Christie: a real murder in the theatre in '53. Can Rockwell and Ronan solve the fatal mishap, while keeping the secrets of The Mousetrap? #WorldPoetryDay
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(2013) Tom Hanks gazes anxiously, from the vessel's bow. Barkhad Abdi's pirates board; he's the captain now. #WorldPoetryDay
(2021) Part horror and part mystery. Couples trapped beside the sea. Ageing exponentially. It's complex but you'll get the gist. And it's Shyamalan, so there's a twist. #WorldPoetryDay
